c74object.name: smartloop
c74object.digest: Finds the longest interval of low-rating bars in a music transcript
c74object.description:
The `smartloop` object binds to an external dictionary containing a music transcript. Every 999 milliseconds, it scans the dictionary to identify the longest continuous time span composed of "Group 2" bars.
Group classification is based on the `rating` key found in each bar: 1. The object calculates the average rating across all bars in the transcript. 2. Bars with a rating > average are assigned to **Group 1**. 3. Bars with a rating <= average are assigned to **Group 2**.
The object outputs the start and end of a time span (in milliseconds) that represents the greatest distance between the beginning of any Group 2 span and the end of any Group 2 span, provided there are no Group 1 bars within that interval.
Temporal analysis requires the `bar_length` metadata, which is retrieved from an external `buffer~` named **bar**. If the buffer is not found, the object will periodically attempt to re-bind to it ("kick the reference").

inlet.description: The object performs analysis automatically every 999ms. No manual triggers are required.

outlet.digest: Outlet 1: Start of longest Group 2 interval (ms)
outlet.description: Outputs the beginning timestamp of the longest continuous interval consisting only of Group 2 bars.

outlet.digest: Outlet 2: End of longest Group 2 interval (ms)
outlet.description: Outputs the ending timestamp of the longest continuous interval consisting only of Group 2 bars.
If the log attribute is enabled, this outlet provides diagnostic status messages.

objarg.name: dictionary-name
objarg.digest: The name of the external dict object to bind to
objarg.description: Specifies the name of the `dict` object that contains the transcript data to be analyzed.

attribute.name: log
attribute.description: When enabled (1), creates an additional outlet for status and diagnostic information.
